paper_title: Embryogenesis and Pollination Experiments in Lactuca sativa

methods.description: This method involves the use of male-sterile forms of Lactuca sativa for pollination experiments with pollen from various species, and the application of chemical substances to induce embryogenesis.

steps.description: Male sterile lettuce plants were cultured in a culture room with a 16 h photoperiod, photosynthetic photon flux density 250 of 300 μmol m-2 s-1, humidity of 40%, and adequate irrigation.
steps.searchable_summary: Cultivate male sterile lettuce plants under controlled environmental conditions

steps.description: For control pollination, fresh pollen was collected from fertile lettuce.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect fresh pollen from fertile lettuce plants

steps.description: Male-sterile plants of lettuce were separated from pollen donors to avoid uncontrolled wide crossing.
steps.searchable_summary: Isolate male-sterile lettuce plants from pollen donors

steps.description: Pistils of L. sativa with receptive stigmas were pollinated in vivo and in vitro with pollen grains of various species.
steps.searchable_summary: Pollinate L. sativa pistils with pollen grains in vivo and in vitro

steps.description: Aqueous solutions of 8 chemical substances, at selected concentrations, were placed with a fine brush on receptive lettuce stigmas.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply chemical solutions to lettuce stigmas

steps.description: Pollen germination on lettuce stigmas was observed under a fluorescent microscope with the use of aniline blue.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe pollen germination using fluorescent microscopy

steps.description: The development of embryos resulting from wide crossing and chemical induction was compared to the control embryos.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare embryo development between treatments and control

steps.description: Ovules were isolated 6, 24 h to six days after pollination for embryological analysis.
Ovules and ovaries were fixed in FAA, embedded in Paraplast, sectioned, and stained for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare ovules and ovaries for microscopic analysis

steps.description: The ploidy of embryo cells was assessed by squashing whole ovules or isolated embryo sacs and staining them.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess ploidy of embryo cells through staining

steps.description: The perimeters of the nuclei of control embryos and of embryos resulting from wide cross-pollination were compared using AxioVision software.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare nucleus perimeters using AxioVision software

methods.description: This method involves observing the embryogenesis process in lettuce after pollination with alien species and chemical treatment.

steps.description: Observe early stages of embryogenesis several hours after pollination.
Record the development of globular embryos and their cell divisions.
steps.searchable_summary: Record globular embryo development and cell division

steps.description: Analyze the presence and structure of suspensors in developing embryos.
Observe the effects of chemical treatment on embryo development.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe chemical treatment effects on embryos

steps.description: Assess the irregular arrangement of cells and disturbances in embryo development.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess cell arrangement and development disturbances
